_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-national autopilot)
_meta.note: Federal House batch: DC delegate + DE-AL + FL-01/FL-02. Two currency catches: (1) Eleanor Holmes Norton terminated her 2026 reelection campaign (FEC termination 2026-01-25, retiring) — record had conflicting status. (2) 'Drew Wilson FL-02' could not be verified as a real candidate: absent from the official FL DOS candidate list for the 2026 general (qualifying open 6/8-6/12), no FEC filing, absent from all local press on the 12-candidate CD-2 field — marked not_running, flagged for deletion as bad roster data. McBride/Patronis/Dunn scored from 119th-Congress roll calls (Clerk XML / GovTrack), Heritage Action, issue-group endorsements, and primary statements.

set.office: Delegate to U.S. House (non-voting) · (retiring · seat open 2026)

profile.religion: Episcopalian (christened St. George's Episcopal, DC; has guest-preached at Washington National Cathedral)
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-09. Non-voting delegate — scored from cosponsorships, official statements, and issue-group ratings rather than floor roll calls. Retirement web-verified (FEC termination report 2026-01-25).

eleanor-holmes-norton.notes_set: Serving since 1991, age 88. CURRENCY FIX 2026-06-09: after publicly insisting through mid-Jan 2026 she would run, her campaign filed an FEC termination report on 2026-01-25 ending the 2026 reelection bid; she is retiring at the end of this term and is NOT on the 2026 ballot (WaPo/NPR/NOTUS). Still holds the seat through the 119th Congress. Signature issue is DC statehood (H.R. 51, passed House 2020 and 2021). Uniformly progressive record: SBA Pro-Life notes she consistently voted against protections for the unborn; original Equality Act cosponsor and founding member of the Transgender Equality Task Force; decades-long opponent of the DC Opportunity Scholarship voucher program (supports charters as the home-rule alternative); introduced a bill to END RFRA's applicability to DC; career defender of DC's assault-weapons ban, magazine limits, and may-issue carry regime; defended DC's sanctuary law and student COVID-vaccine mandate against congressional disapproval. No conservative cross-party deviations of substance found.

0.note: SBA Pro-Life: consistently voted to eliminate/prevent protections for the unborn; signed the 1989 'We Remember' reproductive-freedom statement.

1.note: Career-long fight to strike the Hill's DC abortion-funding riders — documented advocacy for abortion access, the contrary position.

3.note: Actively defended DC's Death with Dignity Act (legal physician-assisted suicide) against congressional disapproval resolutions.

0.note: Original Equality Act cosponsor: 'This bill fills a large space I have tried to fill ever since being elected to Congress.'

1.note: Backed the Respect for Marriage Act; condemned Sen. Lee's religious-liberty amendment to it.

2.note: Founding member of the Congressional Transgender Equality Task Force (2015).

4.note: Equality Act would write SOGI nondiscrimination into federal policy across schools, employment, and public accommodations.

0.note: Decades-long opponent of the DC Opportunity Scholarship (voucher) program; testified for its phase-out. Nuance: supports public charter schools as the home-rule alternative.

1.note: Introduced a bill to end RFRA's applicability to DC, saying RFRA 'is being used... as a sword to discriminate' — direct opposition to conscience-exemption protections.
Championed H.R. 1 (For the People Act), which overrides state photo-ID requirements.

3.note: H.R. 1 champion (mandates mail-voting expansion/drop boxes); introduced a resolution condemning state 'voter suppression' laws.

0.note: Opposed border-wall funding and ICE increases in DREAM Act negotiations.

1.note: DREAM Act backer — supports legalization, not deportation, for those who entered as minors.

2.note: Defended DC's sanctuary law against the 2025 House nullification bill; says ICE-detainer cooperation makes DC 'less safe.'

0.note: Fought concealed-carry reciprocity and carry liberalization for DC; defends the 'good reason' permit regime.

1.note: Career defender of DC's assault-weapons ban and magazine limits against repeal attempts.

2.note: Voted to protect the national background-check system — supports the federal gun-control framework, contrary to NFA/GCA repeal.

4.note: Documented advocacy for strict citizen gun-control measures in DC.

0.note: Repeatedly defended DC's student COVID-19 vaccine mandate against congressional disapproval — supports pharmaceutical mandates.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (ordained PCUSA elder; longtime member, Westminster Presbyterian Church, Wilmington)
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-09 from 119th-Congress roll calls (corroborated via GovTrack + news where Clerk was unreachable), Delaware Senate record, and issue-group endorsements. Reelection bid 2026 web-verified (primary 2026-09-15).

sarah-mcbride.notes_set: First openly transgender member of Congress (DE-AL, elected Nov 2024); previously DE state senator 2021-2024. Running for reelection 2026. Uniformly progressive roll-call record in the 119th: NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Roll 27, 1/23/2025); NAY on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Roll 12, 1/14/2025); NAY on SAVE Act proof-of-citizenship voter registration (Roll 102, 4/10/2025); NO on Laken Riley Act (Roll 6, 1/7/2025). Heritage Action 0% (119th, session and lifetime). Equality Caucus co-chair and Equality Act (H.R. 15) reintroducer. As DE senator: co-sponsored the End-of-Life Options Act (assisted suicide, HB 140) and sponsored SB 2 permit-to-purchase; Giffords-endorsed, committed to an assault-weapons ban. One rubric-aligned vote: supported the Jan 2026 Venezuela war-powers resolution (House tied 215-215) to require congressional authorization. Notable tone deviation (not a vote): complied with the Capitol bathroom policy, calling the fight a distraction.

0.note: Voted NAY on H.R. 21 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Roll 27, 1/23/2025; Cuellar the lone Dem yes).

1.note: EMILY's List / Planned Parenthood Action Fund / Reproductive Freedom for All endorsed — documented abortion-rights advocacy, the contrary position.

3.note: As DE senator, co-sponsored HB 140 End-of-Life Options Act (physician-assisted suicide); passed DE Senate 11-10 in June 2024.

4.note: Accepted EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsements/backing in 2024.

0.note: Congressional Equality Caucus co-chair; among the reintroducers of the Equality Act (H.R. 15, 119th).

1.note: Career advocacy for same-sex-marriage recognition and LGBTQ legal protections.

2.note: Voted NAY on H.R. 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Roll 12, 1/14/2025); first openly transgender member of Congress.

4.note: Equality Act lead — writes SOGI into federal policy across schools and public accommodations.

1.note: The Equality Act she helps lead expressly limits Religious Freedom Restoration Act defenses (bill text) — contrary to conscience exemptions.
Voted NAY on H.R. 22 SAVE Act (proof-of-citizenship voter registration; Roll 102, 4/10/2025).
Voted NO on the Laken Riley Act (mandatory detention of illegal aliens charged with theft; Roll 6, 1/7/2025) — contrary to mandatory-enforcement positions.

0.note: DE Senate sponsor of SB 2 permit-to-purchase (enacted 2024) — the opposite of permitless carry.

1.note: Championed the DE Large-Capacity Magazine Prohibition Act; Giffords-endorsed with a commitment to an assault-weapons ban.

2.note: Committed to stronger federal background checks (Giffords endorsement) — contrary to NFA/GCA repeal.

4.note: Giffords endorsement + 2025 gun-control roundtable with Gabby Giffords — documented support for citizen gun-restriction initiatives.

0.note: Voted with all Democrats for the Jan 2026 Venezuela war-powers resolution (defeated 215-215) — supports the Article I congressional-authorization requirement.

profile.religion: Greek Orthodox (lifelong member, St. John's Greek Orthodox Church, Panama City)
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-09 from House Clerk roll-call XMLs (102, 145, 190, 201 of 2025; 199 of 2026), his Florida CFO record (2017-2025), and official statements. In Congress only since Apr 2025, so several cells rest on no-evidence nulls rather than votes.

jimmy-patronis.notes_set: Won the 4/1/2025 FL-01 special election (Gaetz seat) 57-42; former Florida CFO 2017-2025; running for reelection 2026 (primary 8/18/2026). Heritage Action 89-90% (119th). Roll calls all party-line conservative: YEA SAVE Act (Roll 102), YEA One Big Beautiful Bill both passages (Rolls 145/190 — included PP Medicaid defund + $175B border/wall), YEA Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (Roll 201), NAY Iran War Powers Resolution (Roll 199, 6/3/2026 — backed the executive, scored contrary on the war-powers cell). As CFO: divested $2B in state funds from BlackRock over ESG (Dec 2022); fought the OSHA COVID vaccine mandate; vocal CBDC opponent. NRA A+ ('only one in the race with an A+'). Stated pro-life position includes rape/incest/life exceptions (aggregator-sourced), so the personhood cell is left null. Sponsored H.R. 9009 Firearm Freedom Act (5/2026) and H.R. 7393 (529s for homebuying). No cross-party deviations found on any roll call checked; Heritage's ~10% gap implies minor dissents not yet identified.

0.note: Congressional Record proclamation: 'the freedom to live out one's faith openly and without fear remains essential to the character of our Nation.'

3.note: Spoke at and entered a proclamation honoring the 50th Annual Governmental Prayer Breakfast (Pensacola, Jan 2026) — public-square prayer advocacy.

0.note: YEA on H.R. 1919 Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (Roll 201, 7/17/2025); as CFO: 'A CBDC is the cornerstone of a federal government that could track each and every transaction... no privacy.'

2.note: Mixed record: publicly backs a Balanced Budget Amendment, but voted YEA on the deficit-financed One Big Beautiful Bill (Rolls 145/190) — conflicting evidence, left null.

4.note: As FL CFO, divested $2B in state treasury assets from BlackRock over ESG (Dec 2022), calling ESG a 'social-engineering project.'

1.note: YEA on H.R. 22 SAVE Act — proof-of-citizenship and ID for federal voter registration (Roll 102, 4/10/2025).

0.note: YEA on OBBBA ($175B border security/wall funding) and touts it; CFO-era statements backing the border crackdown.

1.note: NRA A+ rating and endorsement ('proud to be the only one in the race with an A+'); sponsored H.R. 9009 Firearm Freedom Act (5/2026). NRA A+ is hard single-issue evidence against bans/registries.

0.note: NAY on the Iran War Powers Resolution (Roll 199, 6/3/2026) directing an end to unauthorized hostilities — voted against the congressional-authorization requirement.
As CFO, publicly opposed the Biden OSHA COVID vaccine mandate and fought first-responder mandates (scope: COVID-era employer mandates).

profile.confidence_note: Verification attempt 2026-06-09 FAILED: no such candidate exists in any official record. Not on the FL Division of Elections candidate list for FL-02 2026 (12 candidates listed, no Wilson; qualifying window 6/8-6/12 open), no FEC filing surfaced, absent from Ballotpedia and all local press on the CD-2 field. Likely bad roster data (the prominent 'Drew Wilson' in FL politics is a Florida Politics journalist, not a candidate). Nothing scored; flagged for deletion.

drew-wilson-fl-02.notes_set: RECORD FLAGGED AS UNVERIFIABLE / LIKELY ERRONEOUS (2026-06-09). The official Florida DOS candidate roster for U.S. House FL-02 (2026 general) lists 12 candidates — REP: Dunn (filed pre-retirement), Gross, Lewis, Murphy, Norton, Power, Rogers, Rowell; DEM: Bailey, Barnes, Green, Zateslo — with NO Wilson. No FEC Form 2, no Ballotpedia page, no mention in any CD-2 field coverage (WJHG 5/6/2026; Florida Politics debate/fundraising coverage May-June 2026). No withdrawal story exists either — there is no evidence this candidacy was ever real. Marked not_running; recommend DELETING this record after FL qualifying closes 6/12/2026. Do not publish scores.

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-09 from House Clerk roll-call XMLs spanning the 115th-119th Congresses. Retirement web-verified (announced 2026-01-13; still in office, office denies early-exit speculation). Religion not reliably documented — left blank. Prior notes' 'Heritage 85-95% lifetime' was wrong: actual lifetime 79% (119th: 83%).

neal-dunn.notes_set: Physician (urologist, ex-Army surgeon); FL-02 since 2017; Vice Chair, Energy & Commerce (July 2025). CURRENCY: announced 2026-01-13 he will not seek reelection ('pass the torch'); still holds the seat as of 6/2026 amid reported health concerns; no run for other office. Conservative roll-call record: YEA Pain-Capable (Roll 549/2017), YEA Born-Alive (Roll 29/2023), NAY Respect for Marriage Act both votes (Rolls 373+513/2022), NAY Equality Act twice (217/2019, 39/2021), YEA Women & Girls in Sports (12/2025), AYE Parents Bill of Rights (161/2023), AYE Anti-CBDC (230/2024), YEA SAVE Act twice (345/2024, 102/2025), NAY For the People Act (62/2021), YEA Secure the Border Act H.R.2 (209/2023), YEA Laken Riley (23/2025), AYE Concealed Carry Reciprocity (663/2017), NAY post-Uvalde Safer Communities Act (299/2022), objected to AZ+PA electoral certification (Rolls 10+11, Jan 2021). DEVIATIONS: YEA $61B Ukraine supplemental (Roll 151, 4/2024 — 112 Rs voted no); AYE Fiscal Responsibility Act debt deal (243/2023, Heritage-opposed); NAY 2002-AUMF repeal (172/2021). Heritage lifetime 79% — corrected from the old notes' 85-95% claim. NRA 'AQ'+endorsement 2016 (A+ claim unverified); SBA grade page exists but displayed no grade; PP 0% claim unverified.

0.note: On-record 'Dr. Neal Dunn Stands For Life' statement; YEA Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Roll 29, 1/11/2023); AYE Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act (Roll 549, 10/3/2017).

1.note: Votes are restriction-tier (pain-capable, born-alive); no documented abolition advocacy — null per rubric's restrictions-vs-abolition line.

0.note: NAY on the Respect for Marriage Act, both the July 2022 (Roll 373) and final Dec 2022 (Roll 513) votes.

1.note: Voted against federal same-sex-marriage codification (RFMA) and against the Equality Act (Rolls 217/2019, 39/2021).

2.note: YEA on H.R. 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Roll 12, 1/14/2025) — affirms biological sex in law.

4.note: NO on the Equality Act in both the 116th and 117th Congresses.

1.note: AYE on the Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R. 5, Roll 161, 3/24/2023) — school-context parental notification rights, including gender-related changes at school.

0.note: AYE on the CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (H.R. 5403, Roll 230, 5/23/2024).

2.note: Mixed: NAY on the FY2023 omnibus (Roll 549/2022) but AYE on the Fiscal Responsibility Act debt-limit deal (Roll 243/2023, Heritage-opposed) — conflicting evidence, null.

1.note: AYE on the SAVE Act twice (Roll 345, 7/10/2024; Roll 102, 4/10/2025) — proof-of-citizenship voter registration.

3.note: NAY on H.R. 1 For the People Act (Roll 62, 3/3/2021), which mandated nationwide mail-voting expansion and drop boxes.

0.note: YEA on H.R. 2 Secure the Border Act (Roll 209, 5/11/2023) — resumes border-wall construction.

1.note: YEA Laken Riley Act (mandatory detention of charged aliens) noted, but no evidence on universal deportation including minor-entrants — null at this cell's high bar.

3.note: H.R. 2 (YEA) contains the nationwide mandatory E-Verify requirement.

1.note: NAY on the post-Uvalde Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (Roll 299, 6/24/2022 — funded red-flag implementation); NRA 'AQ' rating + endorsement (2016). AYE Concealed Carry Reciprocity (Roll 663/2017).
NAY on repeal of the 2002 Iraq AUMF (Roll 172, 6/17/2021) — voted to keep the standing AUMF.
